skill.name: specdd-trace
skill.description: Trace SpecDD specifications to related code, tests, documentation, changed files, and coverage gaps.
skill.summary: Trace SpecDD contracts across implementation, tests, docs, and change sets.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command execution and weak cryptography indicators, but review found no executable code or cryptographic use. The backtick hits are Markdown references to SpecDD files and a skill name, and the line 3 crypto hit is only descriptive text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misclassified
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Markdown inline code spans as Ruby or shell backtick execution. Lines 14, 16, 22, 23, 24, 38, and 50 contain file paths, a References label, or the specdd-cli skill name, with no executable shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain Markdown instructions and paths. No command invocation, interpreter directive, script, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at line 3, but that line is a natural-language description of tracing SpecDD specs. No hash, cipher, crypto library, or security protocol appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line contains only metadata text describing the skill. There is no evidence of cryptographic functionality or weak algorithm use.

content.value_statement: SpecDD projects can lose clarity when specs, files, tests, and changes diverge. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to map requirements to evidence and identify gaps.
actual_capabilities: Confirms that a project has a root .specdd/bootstrap.md before using the workflow.
Guides bootstrap resolution across project and local SpecDD bootstrap files.
Traces target files, tasks, or behaviors through the effective spec chain.
Links specs to code, tests, documentation, changed files, and missing evidence.
Reports stale specs, untested scenarios, unclear authority, and missing trace links.
limitations: Requires an existing SpecDD project with a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file.
Does not adopt SpecDD into a project unless another workflow is explicitly requested.
Does not grant edit authority unless the active spec chain supports it.
Depends on available project files, tests, and documentation for accurate tracing.

use_cases.title: Review implementation coverage
use_cases.description: Map a feature request or changed file to governing SpecDD specs, tests, and missing verification.

use_cases.title: Audit release readiness
use_cases.description: Find stale specs, unclear authority, and untested scenarios before a release or pull request is approved.

use_cases.title: Maintain requirement documentation
use_cases.description: Check that documentation, local specs, and implementation evidence still describe the same behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Trace one file
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for a single file
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-trace to explain which SpecDD specs govern this file and what tests or docs support it: [file path].

prompt_templates.title: Find coverage gaps
prompt_templates.prompt: Trace the SpecDD chain for [behavior]. Identify related code, tests, docs, missing links, and untested scenarios.

prompt_templates.title: Review changed files
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-trace on these changed files: [file list]. Report each governing spec, satisfied contract, stale spec, and unclear authority.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a release scope
prompt_templates.prompt: Trace the SpecDD coverage for [release scope]. Compare specs, implementation, tests, docs, and unresolved authority questions.

output: Identifies the bootstrap and nearest local specs that govern the file.
Lists the implementation and tests that satisfy each active behavior.
Calls out any missing test, stale documentation, or unclear write authority.

output_examples.input: Review changed files before merging a pull request.
output: Groups changed files by governing SpecDD contracts.
Explains why each file or spec was included in the trace.
Reports gaps where code changed without matching specs, tests, or docs.

output_examples.input: Check release readiness for a SpecDD feature area.
output: Summarizes the effective spec chain for the feature area.
Connects requirements to implementation evidence and verification.
Highlights unresolved stale specs, missing links, and untested scenarios.

best_practices: Start with a clear target path, changed file list, behavior, or release scope.
Use exact SpecDD contract text when making authority or compliance decisions.
Review trace gaps with project owners before changing specs or implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill on projects that lack a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file.
Do not treat referenced files as edit authority unless the spec chain grants it.
Do not infer requirements from similar names, nearby files, or conventions alone.

faq.question: What does this skill trace?
faq.answer: It traces SpecDD specs to code, tests, documentation, changed files, and coverage gaps.

faq.question: Does it modify files?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the agent to avoid edits unless the user explicitly asks and authority is clear.

faq.question: What project setup is required?
faq.answer: The project must already have a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file.

faq.question: Can it adopt SpecDD into a new project?
faq.answer: No. It points users to a separate adoption workflow when they explicitly request SpecDD adoption.

faq.question: How does it handle local specs?
faq.answer: It resolves bootstrap files first, then identifies the effective spec chain for the selected target.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found only Markdown instructions and no executable code, network access, or cryptographic use.
